text: All cats are mammals. All mammals are warm-blooded. Are all cats warm-blooded?
No fish are mammals. All whales are mammals. Are any whales fish?
All squares are rectangles. All rectangles have four sides. Do all squares have four sides?
Some birds can fly. Penguins are birds. Can all penguins fly?
options: Cannot be determined from these premises

text: All roses are flowers. All flowers need water. Do all roses need water?
No reptiles are warm-blooded. All snakes are reptiles. Are snakes warm-blooded?
All engineers use logic. Some artists use logic. Are all artists engineers?
If it rains, the ground gets wet. The ground is wet. Did it necessarily rain?
All prime numbers greater than 2 are odd. The number 9 is odd. Is 9 necessarily prime?
No A are B. Some C are A. Can any C be B?
options: Yes, some C must be B

text: All M are P. No S are M. Are any S necessarily P?
options: No S can be P from these premises alone

text: Some X are Y. All Y are Z. Must some X be Z?
All dogs are animals. Fido is a dog. Is Fido an animal?
Some students study daily. Alex is a student. Must Alex study daily?
options: Cannot be determined from these premises

text: All valid arguments preserve truth. This argument preserves truth. Is this argument necessarily valid?
